title: Scanline — a software 3D renderer
description: A software 3D renderer AND a from-scratch path tracer for the same scene — no WebGL, no GPU, every pixel written by hand in TypeScript. The RASTERIZER transforms, near-plane clips (Sutherland–Hodgman), perspective-divides and scan-converts each triangle with perspective-correct interpolation, a z-buffer, backface culling, two lighting models (Blinn–Phong and a metallic-roughness Cook–Torrance PBR path — GGX/Smith/Fresnel) lit by an analytic HDR environment (skybox + diffuse irradiance + roughness-blurred specular), tangent-space normal mapping, real-time PCF shadow mapping, and an HDR post pipeline (bloom, ACES/Reinhard/Filmic tone mapping, FXAA, vignette). NEW — a RAY-TRACED REFERENCE PATH ('software RTX'): the identical scene is flattened into a world-space triangle soup, indexed by a from-scratch binned-SAH BOUNDING VOLUME HIERARCHY, and rendered by a Monte-Carlo PATH TRACER that shares the rasterizer's exact BSDF — Möller–Trumbore intersection, next-event estimation to punctual AND emissive-area lights, multi-bounce global illumination with colour bleeding, soft (cone/area-sampled) shadows, true mirror & glossy inter-reflection, Russian-roulette termination and the analytic sky as an infinite emitter. It refines PROGRESSIVELY while the camera is still (free anti-aliasing via jittered camera rays) and a draggable SPLIT-SCREEN puts the real-time rasterizer next to the ground-truth path tracer so the shadow-map vs ray-traced-shadow and IBL-probe vs true-reflection differences sit side by side. Plus a pure ambient-occlusion mode, a Cornell box and a hall-of-mirrors scene that only read correctly under GI, an in-app numerical VERIFICATION suite (Möller–Trumbore vs analytic, BVH vs brute force, the sampling statistics, and a furnace energy-conservation test), paste-import of any Wavefront OBJ, PNG export, eight rasterizer debug views, an orbit camera and live stats. NEWEST — a DEFERRED G-BUFFER + SCREEN-SPACE GLOBAL ILLUMINATION pillar that closes the gap between the real-time raster path and the ray-traced reference: the shaded pass records world position/normal/albedo/material plus the lighting split (direct/diffuse-IBL/specular-IBL), then hand-written screen-space passes resolve the indirect light — SSAO (view-space hemisphere kernel), SCREEN-SPACE REFLECTIONS (depth-march that replaces the IBL probe by the real on-screen reflection, so the floor and metals reflect each other), and contact shadows — followed by TEMPORAL ANTI-ALIASING (Halton jitter + reprojection + neighbourhood clamp) that supersamples a still frame for free. Four new deferred debug views (position/roughness/ambient-occlusion/reflections), an Interior showcase scene, and a headless+in-app numerical self-test suite for the screen-space passes. NEWEST — an IMPLICIT-MODELLING pillar: a from-scratch SIGNED DISTANCE FIELD algebra (sphere/box/torus/cylinder/capsule/gyroid primitives, boolean AND smooth-minimum CSG, plus domain transforms — twist, onion shells, infinite repeat) polygonised by HAND-WRITTEN MARCHING CUBES (the Lorensen–Cline 256-case lookup with Paul Bourke's edge + triangle tables), with vertices WELDED across cells into a closed indexed manifold whose smooth normals come straight from the field gradient. Seven implicit scenes (metaballs, a 3-axis-drilled machined part, a gyroid minimal-surface shell, a twisted bar, a blended character, a genus-flipping ring, a carved sphere) with live smoothness/iso/resolution knobs feed the SAME rasterizer and path tracer as any mesh, and an 8-check self-test re-derives the hard claims independently — analytic primitive distances, the smooth-min inequality, the marched sphere's enclosed VOLUME against the closed form, and the EULER CHARACTERISTIC that fixes the topology (a marched sphere gives χ=2, a torus χ=0). NEWEST — REAL-TIME DENOISED PATH TRACING: an EDGE-AVOIDING À-TROUS WAVELET denoiser (Dammertz 2010) with SVGF-style (Schied 2017) per-pixel VARIANCE GUIDANCE reconstructs a clean image from a handful of path-traced samples (often 1 spp) by blurring ONLY along surfaces — stopped at creases (normal), depth cliffs (plane) and detail (luminance) — filtering colour ÷ albedo (demodulated irradiance) so texture never smears, bootstrapped by a spatial-variance estimate so even the first frame cleans up, and variance-aware so it DECAYS TO THE EXACT progressive average as it converges. Primary feature buffers (albedo/normal/position) come from the tracer's own primary hit; a Denoiser panel exposes the σ knobs and a six-way view selector (denoised / noisy / a noisy↔denoised wipe / albedo / normal / variance heat), and a 7-check self-test re-derives kernel correctness, edge preservation, unbiased variance reduction and a real frame end-to-end. NEWEST — VOLUMETRIC PARTICIPATING MEDIA: a from-scratch fog/haze/smoke/nebula layer the path tracer integrates per ray segment — light is lost as transmittance e^(−∫σ ds) (Beer–Lambert), turns by the HENYEY–GREENSTEIN phase function, and the single-scattering albedo σ_s/σ_t is the fraction a collision keeps. Homogeneous media use an analytic SPECTRAL (per-RGB) distance sampler so fog can be COLOURED without bias (an amber smog reddens what it veils because blue is absorbed faster than red); heterogeneous smoke & nebulae use WOODCOCK delta/ratio tracking over a hand-written 3-D fBm density field. In-scattered light comes from phase-function NEXT-EVENT ESTIMATION attenuated by the medium itself, so wherever an occluder blocks the key light the shadow volume reads as a real GOD-RAY beam — shown off by a new Cathedral colonnade and a glowing Nebula scene, with an Atmosphere panel (six presets, density & anisotropy). An 8-check self-test re-derives the lot: the phase function normalises (∫p dω = 1), the spectral and Woodcock estimators reproduce analytic Beer–Lambert per channel, ratio tracking beats delta-tracking variance, and a multiple-scattering FURNACE conserves energy (mean radiance 1.0012 of a unit sky for a lossless medium). Everything pure CPU TypeScript — no WebGL, no GPU.
